data.title: Pentagon dismisses ombudsman of Stars and Stripes newspaper.
data.description: A recent report indicates that the global unemployment rate has risen to 5.7%, with approximately 197 million people out of work. This increase is due to the ongoing economic challenges faced by many countries around the world. The report also highlights that youth unemployment remains a significant issue, with 13.1% of young people worldwide unable to find employment. The report emphasizes the need for governments and organizations to prioritize job creation and support for those seeking work, especially in light of the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the global economy.

data.title: Ace Green Recycling, Inc. and Athena Technology Acquisition Corp. II Announce $32 Million PIPE Investment to Support Proposed Business Combination
data.description: A recent investment in Ace Green Recycling, Inc. has been backed by experienced institutional investors specializing in the sector. The investment is aimed at supporting the development of Ace's facility in Texas and its international operations. The company, known for its sustainable battery recycling services, received the investment on April 23, 2026. The financial backing is expected to fuel growth and expansion in both the Texas facility and global operations of Ace. The specific amount of the investment and the names of the institutional investors were not disclosed.

data.title: NIQ and INTAGE HD Partner to Expand Retail Measurement Across Japan and Global Markets
data.description: A collaboration between NIQ and INTAGE in Tokyo has been announced, aiming to improve their ability in providing clients with more consistent and comparable insights. This partnership will bring together the expertise of both companies to better support their clients. The collaboration is expected to significantly enhance the services offered by NIQ and INTAGE, leveraging their combined strengths to deliver more comprehensive and reliable insights. This move is set to benefit a wide range of clients seeking valuable market information and analysis.

data.title: Analyst predicts altcoins could surge '30%-60%' when Bitcoin hits $86K; expects Bitcoin to stay above $75K despite market predictions.
data.description: Michael van de Poppe, the founder of MN Trading Capital, predicts that Bitcoin will not decrease below $75,000 soon. However, traders on Polymarket are estimating a different scenario.
